issues.comment: stopNotificiationsText property name has typo 'stopNotificiations' instead of 'stopNotifications', causing potential reference errors if other code expects correct spelling.
Template references {{{stopNotificiationsText}}} with same typo as controller, creating consistency but incorrect spelling throughout.
topic.set('details.notificationReasonText', null) assumes topic.details exists, but details could be null/undefined causing TypeError when setting nested property.
current_user could be nil since there's no authentication check before using it in TopicView.new and TopicUser.find_by, causing NoMethodError when accessing user.id.
TopicUser.find_by can return nil if user is not following the topic, but code immediately accesses tu.notification_level and tu.save! without nil check, causing NoMethodError.

Added explicit render :show in perform_show_response method which already renders implicitly, potentially causing ActionView::DoubleRenderError if this method was previously working without explicit render.
user_id assignment uses 'topic' variable when user is not a User object, but should use 'user' since the method parameter is named 'user', not 'topic', causing wrong user_id to be used.
I18n.t('unsubscribe_link', template_args) uses undefined local variable template_args instead of instance variable @template_args, causing NameError.
